checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Re-checked the explicit repo-shape requirements against the current tree with `rg -n "@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card|drawer)" src`, `rg -n "@base-ui/react/context-menu|@base-ui/react/drawer" src`, and `ls src/shared/state`. The revised plan now matches the approved brief's repo-specific prerequisites: `createEntityStore.ts` and `selectionStore.ts` exist, `ContextMenu` and Base UI `Drawer` remain absent, and the added preview-card plus lightbox work is justified by actual raw Base UI owners that still sit outside the proposed `ui/overlay/` boundary.

prior_findings.detail: Re-checked the repository surface with `rg -n "@base-ui/react/context-menu|@base-ui/react/drawer" src` and `sed -n '158,220p' src/shared/components/ToolsPane/ToolsPane.tsx`. The revised plan now does explicitly reconcile the brief's Drawer family with this checkout: both Base UI families remain absent under `src/`, and the only drawer-like implementation is the bespoke `ToolsPaneDrawer` div shell, which the plan correctly records as a non-Base-UI overlay that stays out of scope.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Checked the containment assumptions against the actual Base UI types in `node_modules/@base-ui/react/dialog/popup/DialogPopup.d.ts`, `popover/popup/PopoverPopup.d.ts`, `select/popup/SelectPopup.d.ts`, `menu/popup/MenuPopup.d.ts`, and `alert-dialog/index.parts.d.ts`. The revised plan is technically plausible in this checkout because the relevant popup primitives do expose public `initialFocus` and `finalFocus` controls and the root primitives expose `modal`, so the wrapper layer can centralize focus restoration and modal/body-lock policy without depending on private Base UI internals.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the local Base UI type surface in `node_modules/@base-ui/react/dialog/popup/DialogPopup.d.ts`, `popover/popup/PopoverPopup.d.ts`, `select/popup/SelectPopup.d.ts`, and `menu/popup/MenuPopup.d.ts`. The plan's containment strategy is technically plausible against the actual library APIs in this repo revision because those popup primitives expose public `initialFocus` and `finalFocus` controls, while the roots expose `modal`, so centralized focus restoration and body-lock ownership can be implemented without patching Base UI internals.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Searched the broader overlay surface again with `rg -n "@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|preview-card)" src` plus `rg -n "data-dialog-backdrop|data-dialog-content|data-lightbox-popup|\[data-popup\]|\[data-side\]|role=\"menu\"|role=\"listbox\"|hasDialogAbove|getTopDialogElement|targetHasHigherZIndex|isFloatingOverlayElement" src`. The plan still targets the real shared failure mechanism rather than only the Model Select symptom: it includes the raw Base UI owners, the lightbox topmost/z-index helpers, and the marker-dependent CSS and helper callers that would otherwise keep the old precedence system alive.

prior_findings.detail: Searched the broader overlay subsystem again with `rg -n "@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|preview-card)" src` and the marker/CSS grep from the prior review. The plan is still scoped at the real shared mechanism rather than just the Model Select symptom: raw Base UI ownership remains concentrated in shared wrappers plus `LightboxShell` and one tooltip test mock, while the marker-dependent helper and CSS surfaces are explicitly included for cleanup.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Re-ran the raw-owner inventory with `rg -n "@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card|drawer)" src` and confirmed the revised plan now names the real production and test surface in this repo, including `src/shared/components/ui/command.tsx` for the type-only dialog import and `src/shared/components/ui/tooltipPrimitives.test.tsx` for the raw tooltip mock. That closes the earlier location-coverage gaps around submenu coverage and non-runtime raw imports.

Overlay validation: the new Step 12 wrapper-only grep does now catch the previously-missed plain numeric Tailwind layers such as the current `z-50` positioners in `src/shared/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx:58` and `:106`, so the earlier menu-layering gap appears fixed.

Overlay validation: the broader Step 12 repo-wide layering grep is now too coarse to act as a clean invariant because it searches for any plain numeric Tailwind z-index utility across `src/shared/components`, `src/domains`, and `src/tools`. Running the equivalent command today returns many unrelated layout hits such as `src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx:133`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:606`, and numerous timeline/gallery chrome elements, so that check cannot distinguish forbidden migrated-overlay escapes from intentional non-overlay layering without manual triage.

prior_findings.detail: Re-ran the raw-owner inventory and confirmed the revised plan now names the full production and test surface that actually exists here, including `src/shared/components/ui/command.tsx` for the type-only dialog import and `src/shared/components/ui/tooltipPrimitives.test.tsx` for the mock. That matches the current `rg -n "@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card|drawer)" src` output and closes the earlier location-coverage gaps around Drawer scope and menu submenu coverage.

Overlay validation: Step 12's layering-cleanup grep only checks `tooltipClassName|overlayClassName|zIndexBase|z-\[[0-9]+\]`, but the current menu wrapper's hardcoded numeric layers are plain `z-50` in `src/shared/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx:58` and `:106`. That means the plan's own final invariant command can still pass if an executor leaves the top-level or submenu `z-50` positioners in place, so the validation recipe is still weaker than the wrapper invariant it is trying to prove.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked the current caller chains that still depend on caller-supplied layering with `rg -n "tooltipClassName|overlayClassName|zIndexBase" src`. The revised plan now names the real forwarded-prop paths in `src/shared/components/selectors/ShotSelectorWithAdd.tsx`, `src/shared/components/shots/ShotPrimaryActionButton.tsx`, and `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/LoraSelectorModal.tsx`, so it is no longer limited to direct overlay JSX and should cover the actual call contracts that inject custom layers today.

prior_findings.detail: Checked the current caller chains that still rely on caller-supplied layering. `src/shared/components/selectors/ShotSelectorWithAdd.tsx:192` forwards `tooltipClassName="z-[100001]"` through `src/shared/components/shots/ShotPrimaryActionButton.tsx:81`, and `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/LoraSelectorModal.tsx:111-112` still passes `overlayClassName` plus `zIndexBase` into the dialog wrapper. The revised plan names both of those real caller contracts, so the executor is no longer limited to direct overlay JSX and should be able to remove the forwarded escape hatches as part of the migration.

flags.concern: Overlay validation: Recurring debt: Step 12's repo-wide layering grep is now so broad that it matches many unrelated non-overlay `z-*` utilities, so it cannot serve as a clean pass/fail invariant for migrated Base UI overlay consumers without additional scoping.
flags.evidence: Running the same numeric-z grep against `src/shared/components src/domains src/tools` returns unrelated hits such as `src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx:133`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:606`, and many timeline/gallery layout elements, not just migrated Base UI overlay callers.

flags.concern: Criterion 21: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui, verify_physical_device).
